title: sideritis montana subsp. remota
intro: Sideritis montana subsp. remota is a plant species belonging to the genus Sideritis, commonly known as mountain tea or ironwort, which is native to the Mediterranean region. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits associated with Sideritis montana subsp. remota include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit immunomodulatory effects, as well as its ability to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. The plant can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various conditions, including respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin conditions.
intro_study: According to "Molecules (Basel, Switzerland)", sideritis montana subsp. remota benefits include its main constituent, siderol, which has been found to have cytotoxic activity, particularly against human colon cancer cell lines, with potential as a remarkable anticancer agent.
